title: Paired private pilot: naked model baseline vs Open Scaffold ledger/analyze lane
hypothesis: Under equal model/runtime/budget conditions, Open Scaffold's work-record/evolve loop produces more recoverable, visually coherent, and improvement-directed game artifacts than a naked model loop.

freezeRules.calibrationMutationPolicy: If scenario, scorer, visual rubric, capture seeds, lane rules, or decision rules change after any live result is inspected, affected runs are calibration only and cannot support proof claims.

controls.runtime: same Codex runtime for all enabled lanes
controls.contextWipePhase: after first scored generation and before final recommendation

lanes.label: Naked model baseline
forbiddenAdvantages: compact evidence generated by Open Scaffold
extra human-written repair hints unavailable to Lane B

lanes.label: Open Scaffold ledger/analyze lane
forbiddenAdvantages: claiming controller behavior without a controller

lanes.label: Controller lane
forbiddenAdvantages: pretending a controller lane exists before implementation
mixing controller claims into Lane B ledger/analyze results

pairs.notes: First paired seed for private pilot. Do not publish as proof.
Second paired seed for private pilot.
Third paired seed for private pilot.

decisionRules.noSupport: Call no support if Lane B only produces one prettier output, loses/ties without visual or trajectory improvement, or gains from unequal budget/feedback.
decisionRules.directionalSignal: Call directional signal if Lane B improves one or more evidence tracks but sample size, metric disagreement, or invalid pairs prevent a causal claim.
decisionRules.repeatableSupport: For a real evidence campaign, require Lane B to match or beat mechanical score, win visual/artifact quality in most valid pairs, waste fewer generations after plateau, and improve fresh-agent recovery without hidden advantages.

bannedClaims: Open Scaffold makes the model smarter.
Open Scaffold won because one output looked cooler.
The old 2000m v1 result proves framework superiority.
Calibration fixtures are public contender results.
